stories.headline: Ukraine war briefing: Kyiv lays out how ‘Russian satellites help Iran in war’
stories.summary: Iran bombed US bases and allies’ facilities soon after Russian satellites mapped them, according to Ukrainian assessment. What we know on day 1,505Russian satellites made detailed imagery of military facilities and critical sites across the Middle East including US bases and other targets that were attacked by Iran soon afterwards, according to a Ukrainian intelligence assessment. Reuters reported that the assessment cited at least 24 surveys of

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal a partial win for Trump - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.headline: Oil plunges after US-Iran ceasefire deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The agreement was announced not long before Trump's Tuesday evening deadline was reached.

stories.headline: Trump and Iran announce two-week ceasefire that will open Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Oil prices slide as Tehran says it will allow ‘safe passage’ through vital waterway

stories.headline: Soybean Oil Tumbles After US, Iran Agree to Two-Week Ceasefire
stories.summary: Soybean oil fell the most in three weeks after a temporary ceasefire between the US and Iran pushed crude prices lower, denting the appeal of crop-based biofuels. Palm oil also declined.

stories.headline: Asian LNG Prices Set to Fall After US, Iran Agree to Ceasefire
stories.summary: Asian LNG prices are set to fall after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire that could temporarily reopen the Strait of Hormuz, bringing respite to global energy markets.

stories.headline: Indian Stocks May Extend 4-Day Winning Run as US, Iran Agree to a Ceasefire
stories.summary: Locally, focus is on RBI’s rate decision on Wednesday and TCS’ results Thursday.

stories.headline: Korean Stocks, Won Surge After Iran Ceasefire Brings Relief
stories.summary: South Korean assets surged after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran, easing fears of prolonged disruptions to global energy supplies and triggering a broad risk-on move.

stories.headline: Oil prices slide, stocks surge as Trump announces two-week Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Investors welcome the possible resumption of oil and gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Iran agrees to open Strait of Hormuz for two-week US ceasefire
stories.summary: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says ‘if attacks against Iran are halted,’ then Iran agrees to a US ceasefire.

stories.summary: Celebrations have erupted in Iraq following the announcement of a two-week ceasefire between Iran and the US.

stories.headline: Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller wins election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene
stories.summary: He will serve out the rest of Marjorie Taylor Greene's term, which ends in January 2027.

stories.headline: Top Australian soldier charged with war crimes to remain in jail after bail hearing
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith faces five charges of the war crime of murder, which has a penalty of life in prison.

stories.headline: Asia’s Ceasefire Relief Rally Hinges on Hormuz, Oil: Strategists
stories.summary: Donald Trump’s announcement of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran brings welcome relief to Asian markets in the short term, according to strategists.

stories.headline: Polls in Wisconsin Reflect a More Muted Supreme Court Race
stories.summary: This year’s election has featured more undecided voters and lower overall awareness compared with recent cycles.

stories.summary: Proposed transaction would combine world’s largest music group with a blank-cheque company

stories.headline: China Vanke Seeks to Delay Another Bond Payment to Avoid Default
stories.summary: Developer China Vanke Co. told some bondholders that it is seeking to extend a yuan bond due this month with an offer to repay 40% of the principal upfront, according to people familiar with the matter, as it again races to avoid default.

stories.headline: CBA's Dhar on US-Iran Ceasefire, Impact on Commodities
stories.summary: Vivek Dhar, Head of Commodity and Sustainability Research at the Commonwealth Bank of Australia, discusses the outlook for commodities as oil plunged and gold advanced after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire. He speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Paul Allen on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Treasuries Rise as Ceasefire Spurs Oil Drop, Fed Rate-Cut Hopes
stories.summary: Treasuries rallied, with policy-sensitive two-year bonds leading gains on bets the Federal Reserve will resume interest-rate cuts after oil prices plunged on a US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: Clay Fuller, Trump Ally, Will Take Marjorie Taylor Greene’s Seat in Georgia
stories.summary: Democrats had hoped for a strong showing, but the conservative district in Northwest Georgia elected Clay Fuller after President Trump endorsed him.

stories.headline: Voters expand liberal majority on nationally watched Wisconsin Supreme Court
stories.summary: Wisconsin is home to close elections and contentious ideological battles, and its high court could take up cases on abortion, redistricting and election disputes in coming years.

stories.headline: Republican wins runoff to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene in Congress
stories.summary: Clay Fuller, a district attorney backed by President Donald Trump, defeated Shawn Harris in the deeply red district vacated by Marjorie Taylor Greene.

stories.headline: FTSE Keeps Indonesia’s Market Status Amid Ongoing Reforms
stories.summary: FTSE Russell will “closely monitor” Indonesia’s capital market reforms after the postponement of a March index review of the nation’s equities.

stories.headline: Democrat Wins Mayoral Race in Republican-Leaning Waukesha, Wis.
stories.summary: Alicia Halvensleben, the president of the city’s Common Council, defeated a Republican legislator, continuing a string of Trump-era mayoral wins for Democrats.

Oil Slumps Below $100 as US, Iran Agree to Two-Week Ceasefire
stories.summary: Oil plunged below $100 a barrel after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire that’s expected to halt the American-Israeli military campaign in exchange for Tehran reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Trump’s Iran Threats Look Like Self-Incrimination for Potential War Crimes
stories.summary: President Trump, in vowing to systematically destroy civilian infrastructure and annihilate Iran’s entire civilization, appears to be creating evidence about his intentions.

stories.headline: Bill Ackman proposes €56 billion acquisition of Universal Music Group
stories.summary: Billionaire investor Bill Ackman has proposed a €56 billion deal to merge Universal Music Group with a US acquisition vehicle, delivering shareholders a 78% premium and a potential New York Stock Exchange listing.

stories.headline: Moscow threatens Baltic states, claiming they help Ukraine strike Russia
stories.summary: The Russian foreign ministry issued veiled threats to Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania, accusing them of assisting Ukraine in Kyiv’s campaign against Russia’s oil terminals.

stories.headline: Billionaire investor Ackman makes $64bn bid for Universal Music Group
stories.summary: Universal Music, the label behind superstars Taylor Swift and Bad Bunny, is expected to list in NY from Amsterdam.

stories.headline: How serious will the jet fuel crisis in Europe become?
stories.summary: Jet fuel prices have increased by 95% since the United States and Israel launched military attacks against Iran on 28 February. Higher fares, fuel surcharges and reductions in capacity or limiting of unprofitable routes will be the new normal, energy analysts warn.

stories.headline: JD Vance backs Orbán's re-election bid in Budapest visit and hits out at EU
stories.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance intervenes in the campaign to give Orbán a ringing endorsement in Budapest.

stories.headline: Why Real Madrid tie could make or break Kane's Ballon d'Or hopes
stories.summary: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?

stories.headline: IMF warns of emerging markets’ exposure to ‘flighty’ hedge funds

stories.headline: Democrats Hope to Show Strength in Marjorie Taylor Greene’s Former District
stories.summary: The contest for Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former seat could continue a streak of special election results that have shown a significant shift to the left.

stories.headline: After clashes with Hegseth, Army secretary says he isn’t going anywhere
stories.summary: Dan Driscoll, praised by the White House, said in a statement to The Washington Post that “I have no plans to depart or resign as the Secretary of the Army.”

stories.headline: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
stories.summary: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

stories.headline: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote

stories.headline: Venezuelan Doctor Detained in Texas After Losing Job to Visa Freeze
stories.summary: The Venezuelan doctor, who was forced out of his hospital job by a federal visa pause, was detained by Border Patrol on Monday.

stories.headline: Barcelona’s Flick to defend ’emotional’ teen Lamine Yamal against criticism
stories.summary: Lamine Yamal failed to celebrate in the La Liga victory against Atletico Madrid leading to questions about the winger.

stories.headline: Police destroy opium poppy fields in Pakistan
stories.summary: Pakistani police destroyed hectares of opium poppy fields in a tribal region near the Afghan border, as farmers said poverty leaves them few alternatives.

stories.headline: FIFA opens disciplinary case against Spanish football federation over anti-Muslim chants
stories.summary: Alongside the FIFA investigation, the Catalonia police force has opened its own probe, which is being handled by the Prosecutor's Office for Hate Crimes and Discrimination.

stories.headline: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released by Iraqi militant group, official says
stories.summary: Like many freelancers, Shelly Kittleson often worked on a shoestring budget and without the protections afforded by large news organisations to staff.

stories.headline: Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
stories.summary: The Israeli strikes reportedly targeted Hamas security personnel after they clashed with an Israeli-backed militia near Maghazi camp.

stories.headline: Spain's huge pork industry seeks salvation from swine fever threat
stories.summary: Countries around the world, including the US, have already stopped imports over the outbreak.

stories.headline: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election

stories.headline: AI videos fuel rhetoric as Orbán bids for four more years in Hungary
stories.summary: Videos have targeted Viktor Orbán's election rival, who could unseat him after 16 years in office.

stories.headline: Faced with new energy shock, Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer

stories.headline: Watch: Who is the coup leader who will be Myanmar's next president?
stories.summary: Min Aung Hlaing presided over his last military parade in Myanmar before he takes up the role of president.

stories.headline: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one

stories.headline: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
stories.summary: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?
